title: Membrane form finding
description_for_llm: Form finds a tensioned membrane surface from an initial mesh using Karamba's large deformation pipeline with a soft membrane material, then reports the equilibrium shape. Use for tent, sail, or fabric roof studies where the shape must be found from prestress and boundary conditions, not drawn. Assumptions: input mesh in metres with anchored corner or edge points per the template, membrane carries tension only through its prestress, self weight is small relative to prestress. Limits: this is shape finding, not a full membrane engineering check; wind, snow, and ponding on the found shape are not analyzed; wrinkling and compression zones are not flagged reliably; patterning and seam layout are out of scope. NOT YET AUDITED: the GHX has no ALMOND_IN_*/ALMOND_OUT_* harness params, so run_definition will refuse it. Retrieval context only until harnessed.

inputs.name: ALMOND_IN_MESH
inputs.description: Initial membrane mesh with boundary vertices at the anchor points.

inputs.name: ALMOND_IN_PRESTRESS_KNM
inputs.description: Isotropic membrane prestress driving the form finding.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_DISP_MM
outputs.description: Largest vertex movement from the initial mesh to the found shape.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_UTIL
outputs.description: Utilization ratio per membrane element in the found state.

outputs.name: ALMOND_OUT_MASS_KG
outputs.description: Total membrane mass of the model.
